notes: Forward returns at 5/20/60d horizons overlap across consecutive daily signals; effective independent sample is far below nominal n. Directional evidence only — not grounds for mechanical parameter changes.
v8 telemetry (divergence, hostile_review, regime driver/basis) began accruing mid-June 2026; sections 2-4 deepen with time.
Temporal z baseline excludes the most recent observation; generate-signals consumes the stored baseline next morning (one-day staleness, immaterial at window 60).

tactical.falsifier: A break below the $250 52w low on rising volume, or RSI failing to hold >40 and rolling toward oversold, would flip this to a momentum-down trim.

positional.falsifier: A book-to-bill print <0.95 with backlog YoY turning negative, OR continued op-margin erosion below 9% in the next report, would flip this to a trim — conversely a confirmed B/B >1.10 with margin recovery would justify pushing back toward -60.

strategic.falsifier: A shift in geopolitical regime toward de-escalation/peace-dividend, OR a hard continuing-resolution/sequester event stalling the DoD budget, would flip this decisively to a trim; conversely confirmed margin recovery toward 14%+ flips it to a strategic buy.

tactical.falsifier: Confirmation that today's drop stems from a genuine order cancellation or High-NA delay/China export ban tightening would flip this to a trim.

positional.falsifier: A decisive break below the 50-day SMA ($1639) on volume would flip positional to a trim signal.

strategic.falsifier: A book-to-bill print below 1.0 combined with TSMC AND Intel guiding capex down would flip strategic decisively to trim; conversely a >15% drawdown with backlog still growing flips it to buy.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp break below the 50-day SMA ($509) on rising volume, or RSI collapsing below 40, would flip this to a positive (trim/weakness) read.

positional.falsifier: LIN/APD or LIN/AI.PA spread blowing out sharply (peer premium re-rating higher against LIN) or a decisive loss of the 50-day MA would flip the sign positive.

strategic.falsifier: A confirmed backlog acceleration or price/mix ex-FX turning firmly positive alongside a beat, or DXY rolling over, would flip this negative (buy); conversely explicit management guidedown on margins would push it more positive.

tactical.falsifier: MSFT reclaiming the 50d SMA (~$408) on strong volume, or conversely breaking decisively below the $349 52w low, would flip the tactical read.

positional.falsifier: Azure constant-currency growth decelerating below 22% on the next print, or a capex blowout with margin compression and no revenue acceleration, would confirm thesis impairment and flip this to a trim.

strategic.falsifier: A material OpenAI partnership unwind or exclusivity loss, or forward P/E expanding above 35x with sustained Azure deceleration, would flip the strategic sign to trim.

tactical.falsifier: A follow-through rally of another 2%+ on rising volume with 10Y breaking below 4.35% would flip this to a modest buy/hold-momentum read.

positional.falsifier: Yield spread widening back above 150bps (either 10Y falling hard or ENB selling off), OR confirmation of accelerated LNG-driven gas volume growth, would flip this toward a buy.

strategic.falsifier: A dividend-growth guidance cut, a Mainline recontracting/volume shock, or 10Y falling below ~4% (making the 5.11% yield attractive again) would flip the strategic sign.

tactical.falsifier: A close below the 200-day SMA ($12.57) on rising volume would flip this positive (buy) into a trim/avoid tactical read.

positional.falsifier: GLNCY reversing to UNDERPERFORM COPX by a widening margin (premium turning to discount) plus HY OAS widening past ~3.5bps would flip the positional sign to trim.

strategic.falsifier: LME copper breaking decisively below key support with rising inventories AND Chinese PMI printing sub-48 would signal the trough is deepening, flipping strategic to neutral/trim.

tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking below 30 (flip more negative near-term momentum risk) or a decisive reclaim of the 50DMA at $40.47 on volume (flip to tactical buy).

positional.falsifier: Sustained ETF outflows accelerating alongside a break below the $32.84 52w low would flip this positive (trim), confirming the drawdown is a structural regime change rather than a buyable dip.

strategic.falsifier: Clear evidence the halving cycle is topping (LTH mass distribution + sustained multi-month ETF outflows) would flip strategic to a trim tilt.

tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking below 35 or above 68 alongside a VIX spike above 22 would create a real tactical directional signal.

positional.falsifier: A sustained close below the 200-day SMA ($98.21) would flip this to a clear buy-the-dip (negative) or confirm trend break — either way invalidates the mild-trim read.

strategic.falsifier: A confirmed peso regime shift toward 19+ (Banxico dovish pivot or US tariff shock) would flip the strategic view positive/trim, as USD ADR returns would erode despite operational strength.

tactical.falsifier: A close back above SMA50 ($19.15) on rising volume, or conversely a break of the SMA200 ($15.7) with WTI under $68, would flip the tactical sign.

positional.falsifier: A sustained reclaim of SMA50 with WTI holding >$72 flips more bullish; a dividend cut announcement or break below SMA200 flips it to trim.

strategic.falsifier: Evidence oil is entering an uptrend (WTI sustainably >$85) or a clean pro-shareholder dividend-policy affirmation would flip strategic to a buy tilt; a fuel-pricing-below-parity mandate or forced low-return capex confirms the trim.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close above the 50-day ($12.16) on strong volume with rising freight-rate prints would flip this to a buy; conversely a break below the 200-day ($11.56) would deepen the trim.

positional.falsifier: A GSCPI reading falling below 0.5 (calming supply chains) alongside falling SCFI/WCI would push this positive/trim; a re-escalation spiking GSCPI above 2 would push it more negative.

strategic.falsifier: P/E climbing toward 50x+ (earnings collapsing to trough) with P/B falling below 0.6 would flip this to a strong strategic buy; conversely P/E compressing below 10x on peak earnings would flip it to trim.

positional.falsifier: A negative cRPO print (<18% YoY) or a fresh wave of downward EPS revisions would flip positional to neutral/trim; conversely a decisive reclaim of the 200-day would confirm the buy.

strategic.falsifier: cRPO decelerating below 18% YoY with stalling Now Assist ASP growth would confirm the trim thesis and push strategic positive; sustained 20%+ cRPO plus Now Assist attach-rate inflection flips it firmly negative/buy.
